enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his day off on May 4, 2014: he cleaned his house, worked on request videos, got a call from his friend Aaron about buying a motocross motorcycle, then went with his mom to see Spider-Man 2 and had dinner. He shares his thoughts on the film and mentions dealing with insomnia before a work day.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: enjoyed it but felt the villain's character arc turned evil too quickly; loved the action and 3D experience
intrigued by the idea, especially as a way to access mountain trails before switching to a mountain bike
loves them; the spectacle brings back a sense of childhood wonder

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder Vlog for the 4th of May, 2014, which was yesterday.
Yesterday I kind of, I think I slept in a little bit, because I was up later last night.
Because I knew that I didn't have to work yesterday.
So when I got up I had to do my monthly house cleaning.
I had to clean out the little shavings from my hamsters, put a new shaving in there.
I had to sweep the floor, mop the floor, wipe all the drippy brown crap that's dripping down everything.
You don't even know how it got there.
I don't think I watered the plants, but swept up the porch and then started working on just like making request videos.
It was my day to do request videos.
I didn't even get around, I don't think I got around to doing any work on the website.
I think I did maybe a little bit, but like my buddy Arian called me, he wanted me to buy a motocross motorcycle to get up in the mountains.
He's saying there's all sorts of cool trails you can do in the mountains, figure out the mountains first on the motorbike.
Then go back later on the mountain bike.
That kind of made sense to me.
He says you can get them for like $1,200, so.
Cool way to get, or a cool motorcycle to get for a good price.
I'll be good at him, a dirt bike, like a motocross.
He said he got his for $1,200.
That's pretty interesting.
And then, yeah, and then my brother called.
We were going to go see a movie with my mom, so we went and saw Spider-Man 2, and then went over to the restaurant and had a meal, a couple beers, and then came home.
But I was up late, like with insomnia, and so when I got up this morning, I was like so tired.
I have to go to work here like 25 minutes, and I have to ride like 10 miles, so as long as I sleep early enough.
I don't have to deal with any stress of being late, but yeah, I had kind of insomnia.
I should have made myself sleep, but I don't know.
Um, so, uh, still, yeah, go to work today, and then come home and work again.
Keep working.
Um, what was I going to say?
What was I going to say?
Oh, yeah, Spider-Man.
Spider-Man review?
Uh, Spider-Man was good, man.
Um, I thought it was too bad how his friend has to become so evil, though, you know?
It's like, kind of shit puts a negative light on humanity.
Somebody can be your best friend and turn into your worst enemy.
Um, character arc was a little bit fast for me, quick for me.
Uh, but the action was cool.
Acting was good.
Uh, yeah, it did seem like kind of a preview to the next movie, though.
A little bit too much.
Um, and, um, but, uh, oh man, I guess that's all I can think of.
Um, I love these, uh, Marvel, um, comic movies in 3D at the theater, dude.
I love seeing that kind of stuff, because the action is so cool, and it kind of brings
You back to your childhood when you could, when you're like, you know, wouldn't it be
Cool to watch a, you know, Spider-Man cartoon in real life?
Anyways, um, so that's about that.
Peace out.
Peace out.
Peace out.
